Name: Caterpie
Flavor-Text: Perhaps because it would like to grow up quickly, it has a voracious appetite, eating a hundred leaves a day.

Name: Metapod
Flavor-Text: Its shell is filled with a thick liquid. All of the cells throughout its body are being rebuilt in preparation for evolution.

Name: Butterfree
Flavor-Text: Its wings are covered in toxic scales. If it finds bird Pokémon going after Caterpie, Butterfree sprinkles its scales on them to drive them off.

Name: Paras
Flavor-Text: Whether it’s due to a lack of moisture or a lack of nutrients, in Alola the mushrooms on Paras don’t grow up quite right.

Name: Scyther
Flavor-Text: Its two sharp scythes are more than just weapons. It uses them with dexterity to dress its prey before eating.

Name: Pinsir-GX
Effects: (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Charmander
Flavor-Text: The flame on its tail indicates Charmander’s life force. If it is healthy, the flame burns brightly.

Name: Charmeleon
Flavor-Text: It lashes about with its tail to knock down its foe. It then tears up the fallen opponent with sharp claws.

Name: Charizard-GX
Effects: (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Magmar
Flavor-Text: Its entire body is burning. When it breathes, the temperature rises. When it sneezes, flames shoot out!

Name: Psyduck
Flavor-Text: Using psychokinesis gives it a headache, so it normally passes the time spacing out and doing as little as possible.

Name: Slowpoke
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Alolan home cooking involves drying Slowpoke tails and then simmering them into a salty stew.

Name: Staryu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: In many places, there are folktales of stardust falling into the ocean and becoming Staryu.

Name: Starmie-GX
Effects: Attach 2 [W] Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.
This attack does 40 more damage times the amount of [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Magikarp
Flavor-Text: In the distant past, they were fairly strong, but they have become gradually weaker over time.

Name: Gyarados-GX
Effects: (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Lapras
Flavor-Text: It likes swimming around with people on its back. In the Alola region, it’s an important means of transportation over water.

Name: Vaporeon
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Clean, clear waters are its usual habitat. When it’s about to be attacked by an invading enemy, it dives into the water to hide.

Name: Pikachu
Flavor-Text: Its nature is to store up electricity. Forests where nests of Pikachu live are dangerous, since the trees are so often struck by lightning.

Name: Raichu-GX
Effects: (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Voltorb
Flavor-Text: It was discovered when Poké Balls were introduced. It is said that there is some connection.

Name: Electrode
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It explodes in response to even minor stimuli. It is feared, with the nickname of “The Bomb Ball.”

Name: Jolteon
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card from their hand to 1 of their Pokémon, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its lungs contain an organ that creates electricity. The crackling sound of electricity can be heard when it exhales.

Name: Zapdos
Effects: Flip 4 coins. For each heads, search your deck for a [L] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: A legendary bird Pokémon that is said to appear from clouds while dropping enormous lightning bolts.

Name: Ekans
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: By dislocating its jaw, it can swallow prey larger than itself. After a meal, it curls up and rests.

Name: Ekans
Flavor-Text: By dislocating its jaw, it can swallow prey larger than itself. After a meal, it curls up and rests.

Name: Arbok
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, discard 2 random cards from your opponent’s hand.
Effects: If Jessie & James is in your discard pile, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The latest research has determined that there are over 20 possible arrangements of the patterns on its stomach.

Name: Koffing
Flavor-Text: Its thin, balloon-like body is inflated by horribly toxic gases. It reeks when it is nearby.

Name: Weezing
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is discarded with the effect of Jessie & James, you may have your opponent discard a card from their hand. (They discard that card after the effect of Jessie & James.)
Flavor-Text: If one of the twin Koffing inflates, the other one deflates. It constantly mixes its poisonous gases.

Name: Jynx
Flavor-Text: It sways its hips to a rhythm all its own. The precise movements of Jynx living in Alola are truly wonderful.

Name: Mewtwo-GX
Effects: Discard all Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mew
Flavor-Text: Because it can use all kinds of moves, many scientists believe Mew to be the ancestor of Pokémon.

Name: Geodude
Flavor-Text: Geodude that have lived a long life have had all their edges smoothed out until they’re totally round. They also have a calm, quiet disposition.

Name: Graveler
Effects: Discard 2 [F] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It climbs up cliffs as it heads toward the peak of a mountain. As soon as it reaches the summit, it rolls back down the way it came.

Name: Golem
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 3 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When Golem grow old, they stop shedding their shells. Those that have lived a long, long time have shells green with moss.

Name: Onix-GX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 100 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cubone
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The skull it wears on its head is that of its dead mother. According to some, it will evolve when it comes to terms with the pain of her death.

Name: Clefairy
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: They’re popular, but they’re rare. Trainers who show them off recklessly may be targeted by thieves.

Name: Clefairy
Flavor-Text: They’re popular, but they’re rare. Trainers who show them off recklessly may be targeted by thieves.

Name: Clefable
Flavor-Text: It can’t help but hear a pin drop from over half a mile away, so it lives deep in the mountains where there aren’t many people or Pokémon.

Name: Jigglypuff
Flavor-Text: Recordings of Jigglypuff’s strange lullabies can be purchased from department stores. These CDs can be found near the bedding area.

Name: Wigglytuff-GX
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Heal all damage from this Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mr. Mime
Effects: Each player draws a card.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its pantomime skills are wonderful. You may become enraptured while watching it, but next thing you know, Mr. Mime has made a real wall.

Name: Moltres & Zapdos & Articuno-GX
Effects: Shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck. If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra [R], [W], and [L] Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack’s cost), this attack does 110 damage to 3 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
TAG-TEAM-Rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Farfetch’d
Flavor-Text: The plant stalk it holds is its weapon. The stalk is used like a sword to cut all sorts of things.

Name: Chansey
Effects: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It seems that other Pokémon’s efforts to take its delicious, nutritious egg away from it caused Chansey to get faster at fleeing.

Name: Kangaskhan
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Kangaskhan protects its child by keeping it in its pouch. It has zero forgiveness for those who harm its child and will beat them down.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand.
Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The question of why only Eevee has such unstable genes has still not been solved.

Name: Eevee
Flavor-Text: Current studies show it can evolve into an incredible eight different species of Pokémon.

Name: Snorlax
Flavor-Text: It doesn’t do anything other than eat and sleep. When prompted to make a serious effort, though, it apparently displays awesome power.

Name: Bill’s Analysis
Description: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal up to 2 Trainer cards you find there and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Blaine’s Last Stand
Description: You can play this card only when it is the last card in your hand.Draw 2 cards for each [R] Pokémon you have in play.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Brock’s Grit
Description: Shuffle 6 in any combination of Pokémon and basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Brock’s Pewter City Gym
Description: Onix-GX (both yours and your opponent’s) take 40 less damage from the opponent’s attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Brock’s Training
Description: Attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Geodude, Graveler, Golem, Onix-GX, Cubone, Rhyhorn, Rhydon, or Sudowoodo.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Erika’s Hospitality
Description: You can play this card only if you have 4 or fewer other cards in your hand.Draw a card for each of your opponent’s Pokémon in play.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Giovanni’s Exile
Description: Discard up to 2 of your Benched Pokémon that have no damage counters on them and all cards attached to them.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Jessie & James
Description: Each player discards 2 cards from their hand. Your opponent discards first.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Koga’s Trap
Description: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Lt. Surge’s Strategy
Description: You can play this card only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent.During this turn, you can play 3 Supporter cards (including this card).
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Misty’s Cerulean City Gym
Description: The attacks of Starmie-GX (both yours and your opponent’s) do 40 more damage to the opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Misty’s Determination
Description: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, look at the top 8 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Misty’s Water Command
Description: Move any number of [W] Energy from your Pokémon to your Psyduck, Horsea, Staryu, Starmie-GX, Magikarp, Gyarados, or Lapras in any way you like.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Pokémon Center Lady
Description: Heal 60 damage and remove all Special Conditions from 1 of your Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Sabrina’s Suggestion
Description: Your opponent reveals their hand. You may choose a Supporter card you find there and use the effect of that card as the effect of this card.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Moltres & Zapdos & Articuno-GX
Effects: Shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck. If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra [R], [W], and [L] Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack’s cost), this attack does 110 damage to 3 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
TAG-TEAM-Rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Giovanni’s Exile
Description: Discard up to 2 of your Benched Pokémon that have no damage counters on them and all cards attached to them.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Jessie & James
Description: Each player discards 2 cards from their hand. Your opponent discards first.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Moltres & Zapdos & Articuno-GX
Effects: Shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck. If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra [R], [W], and [L] Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack’s cost), this attack does 110 damage to 3 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
TAG-TEAM-Rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.
